**Description**: A unique identifier for each layer
A data type identifier. Ex: `0 = FP32`, `2 = INT8` (these examples may not be correct).  See `types` below for more details.
The number of blocks/unique segments of data in this layer. Each block could be weights, biases, or something else.
The alignment of the start of this layer, usually 64. The padding to reach this alignment will be filled with zeros.
The count of entries in the map
The offset of the first entry in the map
The total size of the file used for validation
